---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: a785eaa679d9484083023319bb7a5d9c07f6d0c2
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-2's shortName normalization contradicts parent dc-3's definition of full coverage without a declared exempts edge: dc-2 says a shortName collision among truth nodes makes the WHOLE artifact fall to partial, and admits it is 'honestly narrower than flowmap's internal collision-disambiguation scheme' — but flowmap's own renderer disambiguates colliding node-ids in the mermaid it emits, so a pristine generator-produced diagram containing a collision (or containing disambiguated ids that no longer match bare shortName(fqn)) is demoted to partial even though it is entirely 'within the generator's vocabulary', which parent dc-3 defines as full. This is exactly the gap class dc-1 says a judged sweep caught and corrected for %% comments and classDefs, yet dc-2 reintroduces it for node identity; prose disclosure ('disclosed, not hidden') is not a supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3. (confidence 0.55)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-cf-2, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 pins as the meaning of the scope field ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'), on the evidence that both flags' --help text shares an example string. The reinterpretation is argued inside dc-3's own prose but no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-6 is declared; if the two flags' selector value spaces ever diverge (dc-3 itself concedes a --root value --entry rejects is possible), verification would regenerate truth at a different scope than the one the proposal declares, silently violating dc-6's 'regenerates truth at the same scope'. The disclosed build-time-verifiability mitigates but does not remove the undeclared-edge gap. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-cf-3, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-4's witness mechanism (git log -S\u003cidentity-string\u003e -1, most recent fixed-string pickaxe hit over the service dir) can attribute a kept-but-gone element to an unrelated commit — any commit that merely adds or removes the identity string in a comment, test, or unrelated symbol is presented as the named witness with no disclosure. Parent dc-5 chose 'honest witnesses (kept-but-gone plus proposed-new) over concise inference'; dc-4 discloses only the zero-hit case as unresolved, so a false-positive hit is a silently-wrong witness rather than an honest one, and no exempts edge against dc-5 declares this weaker honesty bar. (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8, spec/verification-extractor#dc-1, spec/verification-extractor#dc-2, spec/verification-extractor#dc-3, spec/verification-extractor#dc-4, spec/verification-extractor#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:78a4c4d7c12b7a57263872460a89298f1b2a60671afc23e1c8909ff78b500e92
integrity: sha256:92783e38216baf4c2e280d74cbaeb018647e3053526287c29ab22c6d1e94f1bd
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"cf-1\",\"text\":\"Story dc-2's shortName normalization contradicts parent dc-3's definition of full coverage without a declared exempts edge: dc-2 says a shortName collision among truth nodes makes the WHOLE artifact fall to partial, and admits it is 'honestly narrower than flowmap's internal collision-disambiguation scheme' — but flowmap's own renderer disambiguates colliding node-ids in the mermaid it emits, so a pristine generator-produced diagram containing a collision (or containing disambiguated ids that no longer match bare shortName(fqn)) is demoted to partial even though it is entirely 'within the generator's vocabulary', which parent dc-3 defines as full. This is exactly the gap class dc-1 says a judged sweep caught and corrected for %% comments and classDefs, yet dc-2 reintroduces it for node identity; prose disclosure ('disclosed, not hidden') is not a supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3.\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"cf-2\",\"text\":\"Story dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 pins as the meaning of the scope field ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'), on the evidence that both flags' --help text shares an example string. The reinterpretation is argued inside dc-3's own prose but no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-6 is declared; if the two flags' selector value spaces ever diverge (dc-3 itself concedes a --root value --entry rejects is possible), verification would regenerate truth at a different scope than the one the proposal declares, silently violating dc-6's 'regenerates truth at the same scope'. The disclosed build-time-verifiability mitigates but does not remove the undeclared-edge gap.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"cf-3\",\"text\":\"Story dc-4's witness mechanism (git log -S\u003cidentity-string\u003e -1, most recent fixed-string pickaxe hit over the service dir) can attribute a kept-but-gone element to an unrelated commit — any commit that merely adds or removes the identity string in a comment, test, or unrelated symbol is presented as the named witness with no disclosure. Parent dc-5 chose 'honest witnesses (kept-but-gone plus proposed-new) over concise inference'; dc-4 discloses only the zero-hit case as unresolved, so a false-positive hit is a silently-wrong witness rather than an honest one, and no exempts edge against dc-5 declares this weaker honesty bar.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/verification-extractor@a785eaa679d9484083023319bb7a5d9c07f6d0c2], digest: sha256:78a4c4d7c12b7a57263872460a89298f1b2a60671afc23e1c8909ff78b500e92, integrity: sha256:92783e38216baf4c2e280d74cbaeb018647e3053526287c29ab22c6d1e94f1bd }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-2's shortName normalization contradicts parent dc-3's definition of full coverage without a declared exempts edge: dc-2 says a shortName collision among truth nodes makes the WHOLE artifact fall to partial, and admits it is 'honestly narrower than flowmap's internal collision-disambiguation scheme' — but flowmap's own renderer disambiguates colliding node-ids in the mermaid it emits, so a pristine generator-produced diagram containing a collision (or containing disambiguated ids that no longer match bare shortName(fqn)) is demoted to partial even though it is entirely 'within the generator's vocabulary', which parent dc-3 defines as full. This is exactly the gap class dc-1 says a judged sweep caught and corrected for %% comments and classDefs, yet dc-2 reintroduces it for node identity; prose disclosure ('disclosed, not hidden') is not a supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3. (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-cf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 pins as the meaning of the scope field ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'), on the evidence that both flags' --help text shares an example string. The reinterpretation is argued inside dc-3's own prose but no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-6 is declared; if the two flags' selector value spaces ever diverge (dc-3 itself concedes a --root value --entry rejects is possible), verification would regenerate truth at a different scope than the one the proposal declares, silently violating dc-6's 'regenerates truth at the same scope'. The disclosed build-time-verifiability mitigates but does not remove the undeclared-edge gap. (confidence 0.40)
- **judged-cf-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-4's witness mechanism (git log -S<identity-string> -1, most recent fixed-string pickaxe hit over the service dir) can attribute a kept-but-gone element to an unrelated commit — any commit that merely adds or removes the identity string in a comment, test, or unrelated symbol is presented as the named witness with no disclosure. Parent dc-5 chose 'honest witnesses (kept-but-gone plus proposed-new) over concise inference'; dc-4 discloses only the zero-hit case as unresolved, so a false-positive hit is a silently-wrong witness rather than an honest one, and no exempts edge against dc-5 declares this weaker honesty bar. (confidence 0.30)
